title: Gratitude (Hakarat HaTov): Recognizing the Good in Everything
title_zh: 感恩(Hakarat HaTov):在一切中认识善
title_he: הכרת הטוב: להכיר את הטוב בכל דבר
author: Torah Light Team
primary_sources: Mishnah Berachot 9:5 - Blessing for bad and good
Talmud Bava Kamma 92b - Regarding small favors as great
Talmud Menachot 43b - One hundred daily blessings
Midrash Tanchuma, Emor 19 - Future of thanksgiving
cultural_parallels: 知恩图报 (zhī'ēn túbào) - Recognizing and repaying kindness
滴水之恩,当涌泉相报 - Drop of water, fountain of gratitude
孝 (xiào) - Filial piety parallels honoring parents
Han Xin story - Remembering kindness across years
key_concepts: Hakarat HaTov (הכרת הטוב) - Recognizing the good
Kefiyat Tovah (כפיות טובה) - Denying the good/ingratitude
Nissim Nistarim (ניסים נסתרים) - Hidden daily miracles
Gratitude as foundation for relationship with God
target_audience: New students of Judaism, converts, and those exploring Jewish character development
chinese_cultural_relevance: High - includes extensive parallels with Chinese concepts of gratitude, filial piety, and moral cultivation
